title: SQL Comments
content:
### SQL Comments

Comments explain SQL code and are ignored by the database engine. They are essential for documenting complex queries.

---

### Single-Line Comments

Use two hyphens (--) for single-line comments:

```sql
-- This is a single-line comment
SELECT * FROM employees;

SELECT name -- This is an inline comment
FROM employees;
```

### Multi-Line Comments

Use /* to start and */ to end a multi-line comment:

```sql
/*
 * This is a multi-line comment
 * It can span multiple lines
 * Useful for explaining complex logic
 */
SELECT name, salary
FROM employees
WHERE salary > (
    SELECT AVG(salary)  /* Nested comment */
    FROM employees
);
```

### Best Practices

| Practice | Example |
|----------|---------|
| Explain WHY, not WHAT | -- Calculate bonus for top performers |
| Document complex logic | Explain multi-step calculations |
| Comment performance concerns | -- This query scans the entire table |
| Avoid obvious comments | -- Select all (redundant) |

### Comment Differences by Database

MySQL also supports # for single-line comments. Most databases support both -- and /* */.

```sql
# This is a MySQL-specific comment
SELECT * FROM employees;
```

> **Key Point:** Use -- for quick notes and /* */ for blocking out sections of code during debugging.
codeExample:
-- Calculate total revenue for Q1 2024
SELECT SUM(price * quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM orders
WHERE order_date BETWEEN '2024-01-01' AND '2024-03-31'
  AND status = 'completed';

quiz.question: Which symbol starts a single-line comment in SQL?
What is the best practice for SQL comments?
